title: ABS Approves Blue Ammonia FPSO Concept from SBM Offshore
content: SBM Offshore has received approval in principle from the American Bureau of Shipping (ABS) for its innovative Blue Ammonia Floating Production Storage and Offloading (FPSO) unit, a design aimed at advancing offshore decarbonization. The FPSO will convert extracted natural gas into ammonia while capturing COâ‚‚ emissions through integrated Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS) technology. This creates a pathway for offshore gas to be transformed into a decarbonized energy carrier that can be used in power generation, maritime transport, and the global ammonia market. ABS, recognized as a global leader in FPSO classification, endorsed the design after a detailed review in line with safety and regulatory requirements, highlighting its expertise in managing the unique challenges of ammonia as both a hydrogen carrier and alternative fuel. SBM Offshoreâ€™s Chief Business Officer, Olivier Icyk, described the project as a key step in the companyâ€™s â€œTRUE. BLUE. TRANSITIONâ€ strategy, advancing its net-zero by 2050 commitment. The approval represents a milestone in unlocking new markets and delivering sustainable value within the energy transition.
author: Analysts Account
